# Hanakai Yaku

> A library of 35 atomic skills and 10 orchestrated agents that turn AI coding assistants into disciplined Hanami collaborators. Enforces TDD and DDD discipline for Hanami 2.x Ruby applications.

Skills are atomic building blocks — each one teaches the AI how to do exactly one thing: create a changeset, define a route, write a migration. Agents are pre-wired workflows that chain skills together into complete tasks: build a CRUD resource, setup a slice, implement authentication.

Every code-producing skill contains a HARD-GATE: tests must exist and fail for the right reason before any implementation code is written. This is the single rule that makes the library work.

**35 skills across 14 categories:**
Actions (4), CLI (4), Context (1), Cross-cutting (2), Persistence (5), Dependency Injection (2), dry-monads (1), dry-rb (2), Providers (2), Security (1), Routing (1), Slices (5), Testing (3), Views (2)

**10 agents:**
build-crud-resource, build-api-slice, create-new-slice, hanami-setup, setup-authentication, slice-lifecycle, tdd-loop, validation-contract, add-background-jobs, add-table-column

Every skill uses the same structure: YAML frontmatter, quick reference, HARD-GATE, core process with code examples, and integration table for skill chaining. The `load-context` skill discovers app structure (slices, providers, routes, settings) before any code work begins.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/igmarin/hanakai-yaku): Full reference with skill catalog and agent descriptions
- [CONTEXT.md](https://github.com/igmarin/hanakai-yaku/blob/main/CONTEXT.md): Shared language — 10 core terms every skill uses
- [SKILL.md files](https://github.com/igmarin/hanakai-yaku/tree/main/skills): Individual skill definitions

## Source

- [GitHub](https://github.com/igmarin/hanakai-yaku)
